refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: platformd-trustd.service is a system service that observes the platform's authentication state and produces hardware-rooted attestation over it. It reports the boot state, the identity backing each login session, the lock and verification state of those sessions, and the authentication events that established them, and it signs this state with the system TPM so that a separate party may appraise it. Its state is inspected and its attestation is produced with trustctl1.

The service does not itself grant access to anything. It is an RATS Attester and a local Verifier: it collects Evidence and appraises it against reference values, but the decision to act on the result belongs to the Relying Party that consumes it. Accordingly the service never reports that the platform is trusted; it qualifies every claim as observed, declared, measured, verified or policy-satisfied, as described in below.

The boot state is read from the firmware and the TPM: the firmware type and Secure Boot state from the EFI variables under /sys/firmware/efi, and the measured PCRs from /sys/class/tpm/tpm0. Session and lock state are read from systemd-logind.service8. User identity is resolved through the User/Group Record Lookup API of systemd-userdbd.service8; a record signed by systemd-homed.service8 is verified in-process by checking its Ed25519 signature against the public key carried in the record. Authentication events are submitted by the PAM module pam_platformd.so; see pam_platformd8.
The daemon announces readiness through sd_notify3 and terminates cleanly on SIGTERM. It runs as a system service and requires no configuration.

refsect1.title: The Varlink interface
refsect1.para: The service exposes a single varlink7 interface, io.platformd.Trust, on a AF_UNIX socket at /run/platformd-trustd/io.platformd.Trust. No secret material crosses it. The interface may be browsed and called directly with varlinkctl1.

It provides methods to read the boot evidence (GetBootEvidence, GetMeasuredBoot), the session records (ListSessions, GetSessionTrust), the verified identity for a user (GetUserIdentity), and the recorded authentication events (ListAuthEvents); to evaluate a named policy for a session (EvaluatePolicy); and to produce attestation — a TPM quote (Attest), the runtime authentication measurement log (GetRuntimeLog), a complete attestation token (GetAttestationToken), and the evidence to bind the attestation key to the TPM (GetEnrollment, ActivateCredential). SubmitAuthEvent records an authentication event and is restricted to callers with user ID 0.

refsect1.title: Evidence and attestation
refsect1.para: The boot is reported verified-local when the TPM's PCR 11 matches a reference recorded on the system by trustctl provision, and measured otherwise. The reference is computed from the sections of the unified kernel image with systemd-measure1, the same computation the boot performs, and is refreshed on each kernel installation by a kernel-install8 plugin.

A user identity is reported verified only when its record carries a valid Ed25519 signature. The session's active, unlocked and recently-verified state, and the authentication method that established it, are declared: they cannot be cryptographically proven on their own, and are trustworthy only to the extent that the boot they are declared on is itself attested — the components that report them are measured into that boot.

Attestation is signed by a restricted ECC attestation key derived from the TPM's primary seed. Attest returns a TPM quote over the boot PCRs, bound to a caller nonce and verifiable against the key's public part without trusting the service. Runtime authentication events are folded into an extend-only TPM NV index, whose value is certified by the same key; the log needed to replay it is returned by GetRuntimeLog. GetAttestationToken bundles the quote, the boot measurements and the runtime log into an Entity Attestation Token. A remote party may bind the attestation key to a genuine TPM from the Endorsement Key certificate and a credential-activation exchange driven through GetEnrollment and ActivateCredential.

listitem.para: The Varlink socket the service listens on.
The expected boot measurement recorded by trustctl provision and compared against the running PCR 11 to grade the boot.
The kernel-install8 plugin that refreshes the boot reference when a unified kernel image is installed.
